title: Remove update dates and modified by dates from --json output unless specifically requested with a --json-verbose argument
Fix Claude hooks

title: Add hash to changes.jsonl

title: Add dynamic create behaviour
description: When  leece create is run with no arguments, it should create a new temporary text file in ~/.fleece/create/{random_hash}.txt and open a new text editor at that location with an empty YAML template containing the various issue items. It should then watch for when this text editor is closed, read the YAML and if valid create a new issue with those properties.

title: Remove complete issues from list
description: When an issue is complete, it should not be shown in the  leece list command unless an --all flag is passed in.

title: Add tags property
description: Each issue should be able to be tagged with an arbitrary number of string tags. Please add this property to issues. Tags should not contain whitespace and should be entered with comma separation in the CLI.

title: test issue
description: This is a test issue to test the dynamic issue creation feature.

title: Update version string in CLI
description: The version string is currently just shown as 1.0.0. This should be updated to be based on the version number of the package release. Do this by updating the GitHub actions.

title: Add editor mode documentation to 'fleece create -h'
description: The 'fleece create -h' command currently doesn't show any information on what happens if it is run without any options. It should provide a brief description of the text editor mode in the description of the command.

title: Add tree view as 'fleece tree' command
description: The 'fleece tree' command should be added, showing the list of issues (using the same filtering behaviour as 'fleece list') in the CLI based on the parent-issues property. Just show it as a normal tree using a depth first search. If an issue is referenced by two children, only show it when it is reached in the first depth first search run, but add a small 'virtual' parent indicator above the second child issue to show the connection. Update the documentation to suit.

title: Combine diff and history commands
description: The 'fleece diff' and 'fleece history' commands contain similar behaviour. Just combine them into a single 'fleece diff' command which shows the current history if no arguments are provided but can do a diff if there are file names passed in

title: Add merge --dry-run flag
description: When a --dry-run flag is added to a merge command, it should just show the operations that it will perform and not actually perform the merge. This is for LLMs to be able to make decisions on merging issues without actually running the merge.

title: Add validate command for cyclic dependency detection
description:
Add a 'fleece validate' command that checks for cyclic dependencies in PreviousIssues relationships. The command should:

1. Load all issues and build a dependency graph from PreviousIssues
2. Use depth-first search to detect cycles
3. Report any cycles found with the issue IDs involved
4. Return exit code 0 if no cycles, non-zero if cycles detected

This enables CI integration to prevent invalid dependency chains from being committed.

Follow-up from issue bUkaYe.

title: List now shows all issues
description: List now shows all issues, regardless of whether the --all flag is provided or not. The "complete", "archived", "closed" and "deleted" issues should be removed.

title: Add error messaging for unknown schemas
description: When I run  leece list --all in a project that has an unknown schema in the .fleece/issues_{hash}.jsonl file, it just says that there are no issues. Add more detailed and specific error reporting when a file is detected but the information in the file cannot be read. List the number of rows (i.e. the number of issues) and list which properties are known and which are unknown. This may require some manual parsing of the JSONL.

title: Show command
description: Create a new  leece show <ID> command that shows all of the details related to an issue.

title: Commit and push changes
description:
It should be possible to add --commit and --push flags to the  fleece create command to commit and push changes to the issues in the current branch. A  fleece commit --push command should also be available to do the same thing.

This should just commit and push all of the contents of the .fleece folder in the repository.

If three or more characters are entered, use these to find the matching issue to edit. If there is more than one issue matching this pattern, show these in a table similar to the one for the list command, with a message that there are multiple matching issues.

description: The backtick character  doesn't render properly in the CLI. Either fix this rendering issue by replacing with a single quote ' when rendering (don't change the issue text itself) or work out how to render it properly.

title: Create TUI

title: Update graph structure
description:
I want to make the following changes to the CLI and API to improve the issue's ability to form a directed acyclic task graph.

- Remove the PreviousIssues field - we will move this to the ParentIssues. When migrating, just ignore the PreviousIssues.
- Add a SortOrder to the ParentIssues object that uses a Lexorank string to maintain order between the issues underneath a parent. ParentIssues should become something like this when serialized: "parentIssues": [{"parentIssue":"0xhq4b", "sortOrder":"aaa"}, {"parentIssue":"fsn4js", "sortOrder":"bbb"}]. When migrating, just sort alphabetically and put them in "aaa", "bbb", "ccc", etc. order.
- Remove the Group field from issues.  When migrating, add the group value (if there is one) into the Tags array.

Implementation notes:
- Use a TDD approach
- Update the required CLI commands and documentation

Let me know if you have any clarifying questions.

title: Add series and parallel execution modes
description:
Add a ExecutionMode field to the issue model that can be either series or parallel (default to series). This defines the execution sequence of an issue's children.

Then add a next CLI command that traverses the graph and finds all issues that could be completed next, considering the parents, sort order and execution mode.

Combine the idea, spec and next statuses into a single option "open". Consider this in the migrate command.

Use a TDD approach and let me know if you have any questions about the implementation.

title: Delete unknown properties when migrate is run
description: When fleece migrate is run, if there are any unknown properties found these should be deleted. This can be performed by deserializing and serializing the .jsonl file when the migrate process.

title: Add type switch as -y
description: Instead of having to use --type, add -y as an optional short-form switch for type.

title: Add execution mode to edit and create
description: Add execution mode to text files that are created when the edit and create commands are run. It is currently not shown in the edit command.

title: Next shows parent issues as next
description: Parent issues can never be next up for completion while there are still open child issues

title: Add task graph mode to tree
description:
Add an additional text rendering flag to `fleece tree --task-graph` that renders the structure of the issues bottom up, showing all of the issues in approximate order of what can be done next.

An example is shown below.

The rules are:

- Each issue is shown in it's own row, with one space between rows to demonstrate connections between nodes.
- The graph is organised into vertical "lanes" that show the approximate timing of when tasks can be completed, also showing the hierarchy of tasks (parents and siblings).
- The left-most lane contains all tasks that can be completed next - these are the tasks that are shown by the `fleece next` command.
- If the issue that is next is one of a sequence of issues being completed in series, the next issue in that series should be shown one lane up with a vertical connector directly below the next issue. See the connection between "Toast bread" and "Spread butter" in Step 1 of the example below.
- If an issue has a parent, a horizontal connector should be drawn starting at the issue and travelling to the right until it reaches the lane that is aligned with the parent issue node below it, then travel straight down to the parent (see the connection between "Make coffee" and "Make breakfast" in Step 1 of the example below.

Put together a plan for rendering the task graph as follows and let me know if you have any questions.

## Example task hierarchy

This is approximately what should be shown with `fleece tree` - the dependency tree of tasks.

- Go to work
    - Wake up
    - Make breakfast (parallel)
        - Make coffee
        - Make toast
            - Toast bread
            - Spread butter
    - Get in car
    - Drive to work

## Task order graph

Legend:
- o : task that can be completed next
- x : task that is blocked by a current task
- + : indicates change in direction of edge - render these as the correct Unicode characters for indicating elbows in the graph edges.
- | and - : shows connections between tasks

### Initial

```
o 	   Wake up
|
+-x---+	   Make coffee
|     |
+-x   |	   Toast bread
  |   |
  x-+ |	   Spread butter
    | |
    x-+    Make toast
      |
      x    Make breakfast
      |
      x	   Get in car
      |
      x-+  Drive to work
        |
        x  Go to work
```

### Step 1

```
o-----+    Make coffee
      |
o     |    Toast bread
|     |
+-x-+ |    Spread butter
    | |
    x-+    Make toast
      |
      x    Make breakfast
      |
      x    Get in car
      |
      x-+	 Drive to work
        |
        x	 Go to work
```

### Step 2

```
o-+     Spread butter
  | 
  x-+   Make toast
    |
    x   Make breakfast
    |
    x	Get in car
    |
    x-+	Drive to work
      |
      x	Go to work
```

### Step 3
 
```
o-+    Make toast
  |
  x    Make breakfast
  |
  x    Get in car
  |
  x-+  Drive to work
    |
    x  Go to work
```

### Step 4
 
```
o   Make breakfast
|
x   Get in car
|
x-+ Drive to work
  |
  x Go to work
```

title: Remove parent issues editing
description: Currently the Fleece issue hierarchy is edited in the CLI tool by modifying the parent-issues field. Instead of this, a separate set of commands should be created for modifying the parent issues, such as 'fleece dependency --parent <parent-id> --child <child-id> [--remove]' to add or remove parent issues to/from childs issue. Flags '--after <id>', '--before <id>', '--first', and '--last' can be used to influence the sort order. Keep the parent-issues field for 'create' to allow issues to be created with parents. These commands should run validation that issue IDs exist and that circular dependencies are not created before changing the fields (see 'validate' command).

title: Update JSON output
description: 'fleece show {id} --json' currently doesn't show execution mode. Confirm all significant items are included in JSON output

title: Stop issue ID from changing
description: When the title of an issue is changed using 'fleece edit' the ID is changed as well. This should not happen, once an issue is created the ID should never be changed.

title: Update prime instructions
description: Include next, task hierarchy and tree commands to be improve the prime command output. Task hierarchy (i.e. 'fleece tree --task-graph') should be included in the main prime output text as it is important to understand the context of the current task. Next is not as important as we expect that the agent should have been given a task to complete.

title: Add in-memory service
description: The Fleece API should provide a synchronised .NET service that can be used to load a set of Fleece issues from JSONL files and keep them in memory, keeping the JSONL file up to date and watching for external changes on the JSONL files. See 'https://github.com/nick-boey/Homespun/tree/main/src/Homespun/Features/Fleece/Services' for an implementation of this to be largely copied in and improved.

title: Test
description: Example

title: Extend TUI with full editing, relationships, questions, and advanced features
description:
The initial TUI (from OzBjE1) covers browsing, filtered listing, search, next actionable issues, basic creation (title/type/description/priority), status updates, and priority edits. The following capabilities are missing and should be added:

## Missing Edit Capabilities
- Edit title
- Edit description
- Edit type
- Edit assigned to
- Edit linked PR
- Edit linked issues
- Edit parent issues
- Edit tags
- Edit working branch ID
- Edit execution mode
- Clear priority (set to null)

## Missing Create Options
- Set assigned to during creation
- Set linked PR during creation
- Set linked issues during creation
- Set parent issues during creation
- Set tags during creation
- Set working branch ID during creation
- Set execution mode during creation
- Set initial status

## Missing Question Features
- Ask questions on issues (currently read-only in detail view)
- Answer questions on issues

## Missing Operations
- Delete issues
- View change history
- View task graph / tree view

## Missing Filter Options
- Filter by priority
- Filter by assigned to
- Filter by tags
- Filter by linked PR

title: Add integration tests for in-memory service
description: The in-memory service for Fleece issues appears to only have a limited number of unit tests. Add further testing to this library to ensure that the integration of the queue and service work well. Use mock data to ensure that the tests all function when using issue JSONL files live in a .fleece folder on disk. It also appears that the service queues individual write operations - this shouldn't be necessary as all of the data should be deserialized at once, and as such it would be expected that the queue should operate more as a debouncing system that only deserializes after the latest change rather than a queue of operations. Please confirm whether this intuition is correct.

title: Show issue sync status
description: The status of issues (synced, committed or local) should be shown against each issue when the 'fleece list' command is run by checking the git status of the .fleece folder against the remote and the last commit. If there are changes between either it should indicate whether each issue has been synced (present and identical in the remote), just committed (present and identical in the last commit but not the remote) or local only (added or changed in the staged or unstaged files).

title: 'fleece --version' shows TUI instead of version
description: Running 'fleece --version' just shows the TUI instead of printing the version. Update the command handler to print the current version to screen instead.

title: Review symbols in 'tree' command
description: The symbols appear broken on some terminals, and aren't consistent.

title: Add ID to tree command
description: Add optional ID to 'tree' command that shows children of identified issue

title: Ensure JSON schemas are correct
description: There have been some recent changes to the JSON schema for issues. Confirm that the JSON schemas that are in this repo are still accurate and up to date.

title: Filtering by tag returns all issues in list
description: Filter a set of issues by tag in the list just returns all results, not just the ones with the correct tag. Ensure this behaviour works.

title: Use separate branch for issue syncing
description: It should be configurable to use a separate branch for issue sychronisation to avoid endless commits to main. This can be squash-merged to main every so often.

title: Fleece Server: Optional ASP.NET server for extended issue functionality
description: Create an optional ASP.NET server implementation that the Fleece CLI can connect to for extended functionality beyond what's stored in local JSONL files. The server provides: image attachments, conversations/comments, issue backups, and other rich features. Core issue data remains in local .fleece/ files, but authenticated server connections unlock additional capabilities.

title: Make 'fleece commit' command skip CI
description: Add [skip ci] to the commit message by default, configurable with '--ci' flag that removes this.

title: Add auto merge
description: By default, auto merge the Fleece issues before all commands like create or edit. This should be configurable by global and local settings.

title: Add global and local settings
description: Follow the pattern of having global settings in ~/.fleece/settings.json and local settings in the repository folder. The local settings should override the global settings.

title: Add issue order to show command
description:
When the 'fleece show' command is run, the full sort order and parent issue details should be shown. If the parent issue has a series execution mode show the position of the issue in the series (e.g. (2 of 7)) as well as the issue code, title, status and type for the preceding and next issues.

If there are any child issues, show them as well in a similar fashion.

Consolidate the issue display between this and the issue displayed with the tree and list --oneline commands.

Add this to the JSON outputs as well, but as a post processing step. Do not modify the actual persisted model of the issue.

title: Add permanent deletion with clean command
description:
Keep tombstones in a tombstones_{hash}.JSONL file when `fleece clean` is used. This should be merged similar to the other files. All issues with matching issue IDs should be have their data removed from the issues_{hash}.JSONL. If a new issue is created with a tombstoned hash, add a salt to it and try again.

This only refers to issues with a Deleted status, keep complete, closed and archived issues unless flags are added to the clean command indicating those are also to be deleted.

In doing so check all other issues for similar dependencies.

Implement this mostly in the core API, the CLI is a thin wrapper. Add a note about this to the CLAUDE.md

title: Remove changes file
description: The changes file ends up creating a lot of merge conflicts as the ordering can be different despite the issues having the same hash. Just delete it entirely and trust the issues file for history.

title: Set active issue
description: It should be possible to activate a single issue with a 'fleece activate <ID>' command. This would store the issue ID in an 'active' file in the '.fleece' folder, such that 'fleece show' without any <ID> provided shows the details of that issue.

title: Add 'global' and 'local' options for issues
description: Test

title: Add commands to update status for one or many issues
description: Add a command 'open', 'complete' etc. to update the status quickly for one or many issues. The issue IDs can just be provided as a series of options after the command.

title: Update TUI
description: Create a comprehensive TUI using the https://github.com/XenoAtom/XenoAtom.Terminal.UI library. This TUI should allow searching and editing all properties of the fleece issues in a project. Replace the existing TUI. It should be a thin wrapper around the in memory fleece issues service. The service should load the fleece issues in a project to memory, then asynchronously save the updated issues back to JSONL when modifications are made.

title: Modify task graph rendering to show parallel and series behaviour
description: When issues are meant to be executed in series (e.g Issues 7, 6 and 13 are children of the same parent Issue 5 and Issue 5 is in series execution mode), there should just be a vertical line connecting the issues together. When a line exits a node vertically at the bottom it indicates a 'series' execution, and when it exits horizontally to the right it indicates a 'parallel' execution. When a line exits a node vertically from the top it indicates parallel children and when it exits a node horizontally to the right it indicates parallel children. Update the tree rendering algorithm and tests with this connection behaviour. Note that the lanes are appropriate as is, it is just the connection layout and rendering that should be updated.

title: Add [--issues|-i] <issues_file.jsonl> argument to all CLI commands
description: Add a new argument to the CLI commands that allows any existing JSONL issues file (regardless of name) to be used as the basis issues file to be edited. This is mostly for testing, to be able to use the CLI to display example issues, but should just be added to all commands for future flexibility. If the name isn't of the form 'issues_{6 char hash}.jsonl' any command that edits the issues file (e.g. create or edit) should just overwrite the file.

title: Show next issues with descriptions first on task graph
description:
The next issues are currently sorted alphabetically in the tree "task-graph" view. They should be grouped by issues with descriptions at the top followed by issues without descriptions at the bottom, provided they are not parallel siblings with other issues.

Otherwise proceed with the normal depth first  traversal after the first lane.

title: Adjust task graph sort
description: The "next" issue for task graphs are grouped into those with descriptions and those without descriptions. In addition to this, "next" issues (i.e. the leaf issues of the tree once the completed issues are removed) should first be sorted by whether or not their status is "review" or "open". "Review" issues should be at the top.

title: Show parents in 'tree --task-graph' regardless of status
description: When a parent of a "next" task is not an open issue it doesn't show up in the task graph. The only check on the status should be to find the "next" issues - past this, all parent issues regardless of status should be shown in the depth first traversal of the issues.

title: Add idea issue type
description: In addition to task, chore, feature, etc., add "idea" an issue type. Don't show these in the next command or as roots of the "tree task-graph" command.

title: Ensure issues are committed before creating PRs in prime command
description: Update the prime command to say that **before*" creating any PRs the fleece issues should either be committed with the other changes or committed with 'fleece commit' but with CI turned **on**.

title: Split and extend task graph service
description:
I want to make the task graph generation and graph rendering systems more modular.

The task graph should be constructed from the fleece issues given the parentIssues field, and have additional properties like next or previous available in the issue nodes (but not persisted).

For the "complete" graph, the next and previous are first generated from the series execution modes, and then alphabetically sorted.

This graphing service should then be able to return sub graphs based on queries. These queries should have simple features to be able to filter by status, tags, search title and description, etc. One special flag that will be required for building the current task graph is to filter out all inactive issues (closed, complete, etc.) unless they have a child issue somewhere in their hierarchy that is active (i.e. is in the "next" list).

These graphs should form the underlying infrastructure for all other commands like 'next', 'tree', 'task graph' and so on rather than having custom logic for the commands.

title: Add draft status to issues
description: Add an additional "Draft" status before the "Open" status to issues.

title: Search and sort issues
description:
Add the ability to generate new sub-graphs of the task tree by searching and sorting through issues. The command syntax should be as follows:\

```
fleece list --search <query>
fleece list --next --search <query>
```
The search query string should be used to perform a full text search of all issues and show only the issues found with that query. When used with the --next flag, the tree that is shown should contain all nodes that are related to that query by performing the standard tree and task sub-graph creation first, then pruning off the branches that don't have any results returned on them. When displaying this, the issues that are hit by the search should show up in colour while the related tree issues that are only there for context should be in grey.

A simple search syntax should be used in the search query to allow searching of the other properties of the graph:

"status:open" would search for open statuses
"-status:open" would search for all statuses other than open
"type:bug, feature;" would search for all bugs and features (note spaces are allowed but semicolon must be provided as delimiter

This should be implemented mostly in the sub-graph feature in a type-safe manner, with the CLI just a thin wrapper around it.

title: Make tags optionally key-value pairs
description:
Tags are currently stored as an array of strings. I would like to allow tags to optionally form a dictionary of sorts, where if they are entered as say 'project=frontend', the key is 'project' and the value is 'frontend'. This should allow the search feature that was just merged to use the keys as a way of searching and filtering values, e.g. searching "project:frontend" should give all issues that have that value as a tag. This is basically equivalent to searching "tags:project=frontend" - it more or less elevates the tags to the level of properties.

Multiple tags with the same key are permitted, e.g. "project=frontend, project=services" is OK. The equals sign is required so as not to conflict with the search syntax.

In the fleece issue service a dictionary of these "keyed tags" should be kept as well as the list of tag values.

No spaces are permitted between the equals signs and the tags, only one equals should be provided in each tag, and no spaces are allowed in the key or the value. Keys with names that much other properties is not permitted.

title: Move linked PRs to keyed tags
description:
The "linked PRs" value should be moved from being a special property of an issue to the new key-value dictionary system for tags. If the property is:

linked-prs: 123, 125, 128

This should be translated to the following tags:

tags: hsp-linked-pr=123, hsp-linked-pr=125, hsp-linked-pr=128

This should create in the Fleece issue service a tag set with one "key" and an array of values.

title: Add fleece merge to prime command
description: Sometimes agents that encounter a conflict in Fleece files during a git merge will choose to just delete one rather than run `fleece merge`. Update the `fleece prime` command to insist that agents try using `fleece merge` to resolve merge conflicts with the contents of the ./.fleece folder.

title: Move linked PRs to key-valur tags
Add group issue type
description: Add a new issue type "verify" in addition to the "task", "feature", "bug", "chore" to allow issues to be grouped together under a single task that verifies that the work was complete. Add a note on the purpose of the "verify" issue to the `fleece prime` command.

title: Review graph service outputs
description: With the graph service that Fleece provides, confirm that the graph service by default (with no queries) shows the full graph of all issues (not just the next issues). The `fleece list --next` command should show all issues in the sub-graph that filters out draft, closed, complete and archived issues by providing this as a filter query to the graph service. The `fleece list --next --me` command should add the additional filter for showing all queries that are assigned to the current user, and the `fleece list --next --all` command should remove the filtering on issue status and just show all issues in a single graph.

title: Show draft issues in fleece list --next and fleece list --tree
description: In the fleece list --next and fleece list --tree commands, include issues that are in "draft" status as well. Currently I don't believe they are shown.

title: Update rendering of `fleece list --next` command to show actual execution hierarchy
description:
The current behaviour of the `fleece list --next` command shows an inverted tree hierarchy of the issues. Instead of this, I want it to show the literal order of issues being executed. This is particularly the case for the series execution mode (all issues in the example below are in series execution mode). The order of issues shown is correct, however I would like to change the edges that are shown to indicate that after a parent issue has been modified, the child issue of the next sibling parent should be executed next.

Current:

◌  ncEpMP Child 2.1
│

title: Phase 1: Core Server Infrastructure
description: Foundation work for the Fleece Server including project setup, authentication, and basic API structure.

title: Phase 2: CLI Server Integration
description: Integrate server connectivity into the Fleece CLI with authentication, configuration, and graceful degradation.

title: Phase 3: Image Attachments
description: Implement image attachment support for issues, stored on the server with references in local issues.

title: Phase 4: Conversations & Comments
description: Add conversation/comment threads to issues, enabling team collaboration and discussion.

title: Phase 5: Backup & Sync
description: Server-side backup and optional sync capabilities for issue data.

title: Phase 6: Extended Features
description: Additional server-enabled features like notifications, webhooks, and integrations.

title: Create Fleece.Server ASP.NET Core project
description: Set up new ASP.NET Core Web API project (Fleece.Server) in the solution. Use minimal API or controllers pattern. Include: project structure, dependency injection setup, configuration system, and basic health check endpoint.

title: Design server data storage architecture
description: Design database schema and storage approach for server-side data. Options: SQLite for simplicity, PostgreSQL for production. Design tables for: attachments, comments, user accounts, repository registrations, audit logs. Consider Entity Framework Core or Dapper.

title: Implement authentication system
description: Implement user authentication for the server. Support: API key authentication for CLI, optional OAuth/OIDC for web UI. Include user registration, token management, and secure credential storage. Consider ASP.NET Core Identity or simpler custom approach.

title: Implement authorization and permissions
description: Design permission model for server resources. Repository-level access control: owner, collaborator, viewer roles. Per-issue permissions optional. Integrate with authentication system.

title: Create repository registration API
description: API endpoints to register/link local repositories with the server. Track repository metadata, generate unique repo identifiers that link local .fleece/ data to server-side extensions. Support multiple repos per user.

title: Add server-side logging and monitoring
description: Implement structured logging (Serilog), health checks, and basic metrics. Prepare for production deployment with proper observability.

title: Create Fleece.Client library for server communication
description: New library project (Fleece.Client) with HTTP client for server API communication. Typed client with strongly-typed request/response models. Handle retries, timeouts, and error responses gracefully.

title: Add server configuration to CLI
description: Add configuration options for server URL and credentials. Support: global config (~/.fleece/config), per-repo config (.fleece/config), environment variables. Commands: 'fleece server configure', 'fleece server status'.

title: Implement CLI authentication commands
description: Add 'fleece auth login', 'fleece auth logout', 'fleece auth status' commands. Securely store credentials (OS keychain preferred, fallback to encrypted file). Support API key and interactive login flows.

title: Implement graceful degradation pattern
description: Ensure CLI works fully offline. Server features degrade gracefully when: server unreachable, not authenticated, or server disabled. Clear messaging about which features require server. Never block core local functionality.

title: Add repository linking commands
description: Commands to link local repo with server: 'fleece server link', 'fleece server unlink'. Store server repo ID in local config. Verify server connectivity and permissions during link.

title: Design attachment storage system
description: Design server-side file storage for attachments. Options: local filesystem, S3-compatible object storage, Azure Blob. Store metadata (filename, size, mime type, uploader, timestamp) in database. Generate unique attachment IDs.

title: Implement attachment upload API
description: Server endpoints for uploading attachments: POST /api/repos/{repoId}/issues/{issueId}/attachments. Support multipart uploads, size limits, allowed file types. Return attachment metadata including URL/ID.

title: Implement attachment retrieval API
description: Server endpoints for viewing/downloading attachments: GET /api/attachments/{id}, LIST /api/repos/{repoId}/issues/{issueId}/attachments. Support thumbnails for images, signed URLs for security.

title: Add CLI attachment commands
description: CLI commands: 'fleece attach <issue-id> <file>', 'fleece attachments <issue-id>', 'fleece attachment download <attachment-id>'. Show attachment info in 'fleece show' when server connected.

title: Add image processing and optimization
description: Server-side image processing: generate thumbnails, optimize for web, extract EXIF data. Use ImageSharp or SkiaSharp. Store multiple sizes (thumbnail, preview, original).

title: Design comment data model
description: Design database schema for comments/conversations. Fields: id, issueId, repoId, authorId, content (markdown), createdAt, updatedAt, parentCommentId (for threading). Support edits and soft deletes.

title: Implement comments API
description: Server endpoints: POST/GET/PUT/DELETE /api/repos/{repoId}/issues/{issueId}/comments. Support pagination, threading (replies), and markdown rendering. Include author info in responses.

title: Add CLI comment commands
description: CLI commands: 'fleece comment <issue-id> [message]' (opens editor if no message), 'fleece comments <issue-id>' (list comments). Show recent comments in 'fleece show' when server connected.

title: Support @mentions in comments
description: Parse @username mentions in comments. Store mentions in database for notification system. Validate mentioned users exist. Render mentions as links in output.

title: Add comment reactions/emoji
description: Support emoji reactions on comments (thumbs up, etc). Simple reaction API. Display reaction counts in CLI output. Keep implementation lightweight.

title: Design backup data model
description: Design schema for storing issue backups. Store: full issue snapshots, backup timestamp, repo version/commit. Support incremental backups. Track backup history and allow point-in-time restore.

title: Implement backup API
description: Server endpoints: POST /api/repos/{repoId}/backups (trigger backup), GET /api/repos/{repoId}/backups (list backups), GET /api/repos/{repoId}/backups/{id} (get specific backup). Support scheduled automatic backups.

title: Add CLI backup commands
description: CLI commands: 'fleece backup' (push current state to server), 'fleece backups' (list server backups), 'fleece restore <backup-id>' (restore from backup). Add --dry-run for restore.

title: Implement automatic backup scheduling
description: Server-side scheduled backups: configurable frequency per repo, retention policies (keep last N backups, keep daily/weekly/monthly). Background job processing with Hangfire or similar.

title: Add issue history/audit trail
description: Track all issue changes on server: who changed what, when. Store diffs or full snapshots. API to view issue history. Show in 'fleece show --history' when server connected.

title: Implement notification system
description: Server-side notification system: track events (mentions, comments, issue updates), store notifications per user, mark as read/unread. API: GET /api/notifications, PUT /api/notifications/{id}/read.

title: Add webhook support
description: Allow repos to register webhook URLs for events (issue created, status changed, comment added). Configurable per-repo. Signed payloads for security. Retry on failure.

title: Build optional web dashboard
description: Blazor or React SPA for viewing/managing issues in browser. Read-only initially: view issues, comments, attachments. Later: editing, creating issues. Complements CLI, doesn't replace it.

title: Add email notifications
description: Optional email notifications for subscribed events. User preferences for notification frequency (immediate, daily digest, weekly). Email templates for different event types.

title: Implement team/organization support
description: Group users into teams/organizations. Org-level permissions, shared repos within org. Team mentions in comments. Admin roles for user management.

title: Add search across server data
description: Full-text search for comments, attachment metadata. API endpoint for searching. Integrate with CLI 'fleece search' when server connected. Consider Elasticsearch for scale or simple SQL full-text.

title: Update issue merging to prevent PR change pollution
description: The issues_{hash}.jsonl system results in very large diffs - when issues get modified this often gets picked up as a complete file recreation rather than just a change (i.e. a rename with additional lines added), and the resulting change adds a lot to the number of lines modified in the PR. Instead of this, I want to come up with a system that avoids a very large number of changes.

title: Review and fix diff command
description:
I have added two issues files @tests/examples/diff-issues/issues_22a70a.jsonl and @tests/examples/diff-issues/issues_3177dd.jsonl. In this folder, I can run `fleece list --issues issues_22a70a.jsonl` for both of these files but running `fleece diff issues_22a70a.jsonl issues_3177dd.jsonl` fails with `Error: Encountered malformed markup tag at position 36.`

title: Fix tagging system
description:
The keyed tag system is meant to have tags added to the issues with key=value pairings. I can successfully add a tag as follows: `fleece edit <id> --tag key=value`.

However, when I search for all tags with that key, this doesn't work: `fleece list --tag key`, as it instead returns `Error: Issue 'key' not found`. It will only return the correct result if I search `fleece list --tag key=value` or `fleece list --tag-key key=value`.

I want to remove the --tag-key switch and instead just have --tag. This should allow for both cases, where `--tag key` shows all issues with that key regardless of the value, and `--tag key=value` shows all issues with that key **and** value matched.

Update the documentation to include information about the tagging system.

title: Fix last update value for execution mode
Fix merging of execution mode when last updated time is not included
description:
I am finding some bugs in the persistence of changes to an issue's execution mode, when changes are being merged in. This should be being handled by the IssueMerger - when the execution mode has been set once I don't expect that merging in issues where no changes have been made to the execution mode would alter it.

However, when I look at the issues that are coming in and affecting it, it appears that they do not have an ExecutionModeLastUpdate field that is set within the JSONL file (or at least after merging there is nothing set there). Review the merging behaviour to see if you can find what is causing this bug to occur.

title: Review graph retrieval, sorting and ordering system
description:
The `fleece list --next` command renders a graph of issues in order of execution. This relies on a number of internal services to 1) create the graph or sub-graph of issues, 2) construct a lane-based layout of these issues such that each issue is on a row and the node representing the issue is in a particular lane then 3) rendering the issue graph itself with the connecting edges between the layers.

Some of these services are exposed by the Fleece.Core library which is consumed by other applications to allow rendering of the graph using a UI. There is currently a very particular way that the issues are ordered which are affecting these external UIs, which are designed to be configurable. The ordering sequence at the moment is somewhat complex, with the 'actionable' issues in lane 0 being ordered first by whether they have a description but then also considering things like status, priority, parallelism, ordering by lexical order and so on.

I would like to make this entirely configurable if it is not already. It should ideally be such that a set of rules is applied to the ordering of the root 'actionable' issues in lane 0. This should default to oldest at the top to newest at the bottom, but can be configured to be by priority or by containing description or as combinations of these sort orders. The remaining rules that branch off these should always be ordered in the exact same way - by lexical order of their primary parent issue (the first issue that is in their list of parents).

Lexical ordering is currently optional - I would like to change this such that it is not optional, and lexical order is always required or automatically applied. If a set of issues is read in without lexical ordering applied, they should be sorted alphabetically and then have this lexical ordering applied such that when they are serialized again all parent assignments have a lexical order.

The other aspect of visibility is whether to show certain statuses at all. Currently complete, closed and archived issues (the 'inactive' statuses) are not show at all. There should be an option to show any issues with these statuses as well, with two possible overall conditions that apply to the whole graph generated: show only if they have children (at any depth) with 'active' statuses (draft, open, progress, review) or always show. This configuration should be separate to the statuses that are shown.

Configuring this visibility should result in different sub-graphs being generated, after which the graph lane-based layout algorithm takes over.

title: Make lexical ordering mandatory and auto-applied
description:
## Summary

Lexical ordering (SortOrder on ParentIssueRef) is currently optional. This task makes it mandatory and auto-applied so that all parent-child relationships always have a consistent lexical order.

## Requirements

1. **Auto-assign SortOrder on load**: When issues are loaded from storage and a `ParentIssueRef` lacks a `SortOrder` (null or empty), automatically assign one based on alphabetical title ordering among siblings under the same parent.

2. **Persist auto-assigned order**: When issues with newly-assigned SortOrder values are serialized back to storage (e.g., during any write operation), the auto-assigned lexical order should be saved so subsequent reads don't need to recompute.

3. **Remove optional behavior**: Remove any code paths in `IssueService`, `DependencyService`, or related services that treat SortOrder as optional or fall back to other sorting criteria when SortOrder is missing.

4. **Use LexoRank for assignment**: Use the existing `LexoRank.GenerateInitialRanks()` utility to generate sort orders when auto-assigning.

## Key Files to Modify

- `src/Fleece.Core/Services/IssueService.cs` — `BuildChildrenLookup()` (line ~1354) currently has fallback sorting when SortOrder is missing. Update to ensure SortOrder is always present.
- `src/Fleece.Core/Services/StorageService.cs` — Add auto-assignment logic when loading issues, or in a normalization step.
- `src/Fleece.Core/Models/ParentIssueRef.cs` — Verify SortOrder is always populated.
- `src/Fleece.Core/Utilities/LexoRank.cs` — Existing utility, no changes expected.

## Testing

- Add unit tests confirming that issues loaded without SortOrder get one assigned alphabetically.
- Add unit tests confirming that persisted SortOrder values are stable across load/save cycles.
- Verify existing tests still pass (SortOrder should already be present in well-formed test data).

## Acceptance Criteria

- No `ParentIssueRef` in the system ever has a null/empty SortOrder after loading.
- Children under a parent are always sorted by SortOrder first, with no secondary fallback needed.
- Auto-assigned order matches alphabetical title ordering among siblings.

title: Make lane 0 actionable issue sorting configurable
description:
## Summary

The sorting of lane 0 (actionable/root) issues in the task graph is currently hard-coded. This task makes it configurable with a composable set of sort rules, defaulting to oldest-first.

## Requirements

1. **Create a sort configuration model**: Define a `GraphSortCriteria` enum or similar with values: `CreatedAt`, `Priority`, `HasDescription`, `Title`. Create a `GraphSortRule` model that pairs a criterion with a direction (ascending/descending). The sort configuration is an ordered list of `GraphSortRule` items.

2. **Default sort order**: The default should be `CreatedAt` ascending (oldest at top, newest at bottom). This replaces the current default of priority → description presence → title.

3. **Apply sort configuration to lane 0 issues**: Update `BuildTaskGraphLayoutAsync()` (line ~636) to use the configurable sort rules for ordering root issues instead of the hard-coded `.OrderBy()` chain.

4. **Apply to GetNextIssuesAsync**: Update `GetNextIssuesAsync()` (line ~557) similarly so that the `next` command respects the same configurable sort order.

5. **Non-lane-0 children always sort by lexical order**: Ensure that `BuildChildrenLookup()` always sorts children by their `SortOrder` (lexical order from primary parent ref) as the sole criterion. Remove secondary sort criteria (status, description, priority, title) from child ordering — lexical order is now mandatory (per sub-issue 1).

6. **Expose via Core API**: Add sort configuration parameter to `BuildTaskGraphLayoutAsync`, `GetNextIssuesAsync`, and `QueryGraphAsync` in `IIssueService`. Use the default sort when not specified.

7. **Expose via CLI**: Add `--sort` flag to `list --next` and `next` commands accepting comma-separated criteria like `--sort created,priority` or `--sort priority:desc,title:asc`. Document the available criteria.

## Key Files to Modify

- `src/Fleece.Core/Models/` — Add new `GraphSortCriteria`, `GraphSortRule`, `GraphSortConfig` models.
- `src/Fleece.Core/Services/Interfaces/IIssueService.cs` — Add sort config parameters to graph methods.
- `src/Fleece.Core/Services/IssueService.cs` — Refactor `BuildTaskGraphLayoutAsync()`, `GetNextIssuesAsync()`, and `BuildChildrenLookup()` sorting logic.
- `src/Fleece.Cli/Settings/ListSettings.cs` — Add `--sort` option.
- `src/Fleece.Cli/Settings/NextSettings.cs` — Add `--sort` option.
- `src/Fleece.Cli/Commands/ListCommand.cs` — Pass sort config to Core API.
- `src/Fleece.Cli/Commands/NextCommand.cs` — Pass sort config to Core API.

## Testing

- Unit tests for each sort criterion individually and in combination.
- Test that default sort (oldest-first) works correctly.
- Test that children always sort by lexical order regardless of sort config.
- Verify CLI parsing of `--sort` flag with various formats.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Lane 0 issues are sorted by configurable criteria, defaulting to oldest-first.
- Non-lane-0 children are always sorted by lexical order only.
- External consumers can pass sort configuration through the Core API.
- CLI `--sort` flag works on `list --next` and `next` commands.

title: Add configurable inactive status visibility for graph generation
description:
## Summary

Currently, terminal-status issues (complete, closed, archived) are either fully hidden or fully shown via the `includeTerminal` boolean. This task adds a more granular visibility configuration with three modes that control which inactive issues appear in the generated graph.

## Requirements

1. **Define visibility modes**: Create an enum `InactiveVisibility` with values:
   - `Hide` — Current default. Terminal-status issues are excluded from the graph entirely (unless they are ancestors of active issues needed for hierarchy context).
   - `IfHasActiveDescendants` — Show terminal-status issues only if they have at least one descendant (at any depth) with an active status (Draft, Open, Progress, Review).
   - `Always` — Show all terminal-status issues regardless of whether they have active descendants.

2. **Apply visibility to sub-graph generation**: Modify `BuildTaskGraphLayoutAsync()` (line ~590) to accept the new visibility mode instead of (or in addition to) the boolean `includeTerminal`. The visibility mode determines which issues are included in the graph BEFORE the lane-based layout algorithm runs.

3. **Replace includeTerminal parameter**: Update `IIssueService.BuildTaskGraphLayoutAsync`, `QueryGraphAsync`, and related methods to accept `InactiveVisibility` instead of `bool includeTerminal`. Maintain backward compatibility by treating `true` as `Always` and `false` as `Hide` if needed during transition.

4. **Implement IfHasActiveDescendants logic**: When this mode is active, traverse the issue hierarchy depth-first to determine which terminal issues have active descendants. Include those terminal issues (and their ancestor chain) in the graph. This is similar to the existing ancestor-collection logic (line ~619 `CollectIssuesToDisplay`) but working in the opposite direction — from terminal parents down to active children.

5. **Keep visibility separate from status filtering**: This visibility configuration applies to the graph generation only. It should NOT affect `FilterAsync` or `list` command filtering — those remain controlled by existing `--all` / `--status` flags.

6. **Expose via Core API**: Add `InactiveVisibility` parameter to graph-building methods in `IIssueService`.

7. **Expose via CLI**: Add `--show-inactive` flag to `list --next` and `next` commands accepting values: `hide`, `if-active-children`, `always`. Default: `hide`.

## Key Files to Modify

- `src/Fleece.Core/Models/` — Add `InactiveVisibility` enum.
- `src/Fleece.Core/Services/Interfaces/IIssueService.cs` — Update graph method signatures.
- `src/Fleece.Core/Services/IssueService.cs` — Modify `BuildTaskGraphLayoutAsync()`, `CollectIssuesToDisplay()`, and `BuildFilteredTaskGraphLayoutAsync()` to respect visibility mode.
- `src/Fleece.Cli/Settings/ListSettings.cs` — Add `--show-inactive` option.
- `src/Fleece.Cli/Settings/NextSettings.cs` — Add `--show-inactive` option.
- `src/Fleece.Cli/Commands/ListCommand.cs` — Pass visibility mode to Core API.
- `src/Fleece.Cli/Commands/NextCommand.cs` — Pass visibility mode to Core API.

## Testing

- Unit test `Hide` mode matches current behavior.
- Unit test `IfHasActiveDescendants` correctly includes terminal parents with active children and excludes those without.
- Unit test `Always` includes all terminal issues.
- Test deep hierarchies (grandparent terminal → parent terminal → child active).
- Verify layout algorithm handles mixed active/inactive nodes correctly.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Three visibility modes available and working correctly.
- Default behavior (`Hide`) is unchanged from current behavior.
- `IfHasActiveDescendants` correctly traverses the full depth of the hierarchy.
- Visibility is independent of status filtering on `list` command.
- CLI `--show-inactive` flag works on `list --next` and `next` commands.

title: Create FleeceSpec feature
description: I want to create a FleeceSpec feature that has a two-way relationship between a tree of issues and a single document-like specification.

title: Remove questions and answers from Fleece issues
description: The questions and answers feature of Fleece issues is entirely unused by the user base. Remove this functionality from the schema, CLI, Core services and documentation. Don't worry about backwards compatibility.

title: Remove support for multiple parents
description: Currently, issues can form a graph due to the fact that any issue can have multiple parents. I want to remove this feature such that any issue can only have one parent (with an enforced lexical order). Update the JSONL schema for this, and create a migration such that when any issue file is read with the old schema (multiple parent issues) only the first parent is kept and lexical ordering is added (this was already imposed by an update in a previous PR). This migration should extend to the actual service itself - when the service is run that loads in a fleece file, it should be automatically migrated.

title: Show draft issue status and idea issue type in the default `fleece list --next` graph
description: Currently issues that have the status "draft" and the type "idea" aren't being shown with the default settings for the graph shown with `fleece list --next`. They should be shown.

title: Add ability to move issues up and down list as well as initial parent creation ranking
description:
Review the lexical ranking behaviour.

When an issue is created and moved to be a child of another issue (which previously had no children) in an application that uses the Fleece.Core API, it starts off with a lexorank of 0. When I add another issue below it, this is given the rank 0V. Using the move up and move down features of the application just adds more V's to the end of the lexorank. I believe this may be because the Fleece.Core library does not provide methods for moving issues up or down the list of siblings of a parent and the methods implemented by the application are flawed, but ensure that this behaviour is not coming from the Fleece library.

Instead of this, it should be starting with the lexorank aaa, then when a new issue is added below it that should be given the rank bbb and so on. If a new issue is added between them this would have ranking aaab and so on.

Add API methods to move issues up and down the list of siblings by updating the lexical ranking of only the issue selected. This is unless the lexical ranking of the sibling above it is `a`, in which case all sibling rankings should be normalised first to aaa, bbb, etc. These methods should return a result indicating whether the issue has moved up or down the list or the move was invalid (with reasons for why it was invalid).

Generally, lexoranks should be alphanumeric and lowercase only.

Also ensure that the methods used by the CLI for changing dependencies with specific orders (`--after <id>`, `--first`, `--last`, etc.) are exposed via the API.

title: Add multiple parent issues in every location
description:
If an issue has multiple parents, it should show up in the location for each of the parents when rendered with `fleece list --tree` or `fleece list --next`. Modify the underlying graph service to ensure that this happens - the graph should only contain a reference to the issue so it should reference the same issue.

When rendered it should show an indication of how many times it is rendered in the list e.g. "abc123 Issue Title (1/3)" the first time it is rendered, and (2/3), etc. the remaining times it is rendered (from top to bottom). The first number is the time it is rendered and the second number is the total number of parents that the issue has that are shown in the overall graph (not limited by the graph depth shown, the full list of the filtered subgraph).

This should be exposed as a property in the graph layout that is output via the Fleece.Core API.

title: Fix transitive dependencies with multiple parents
Integrate Fleece issues with Openspec changes
Transition to functional core, imperative service architecture
description:
## Specification: Functional Core, Imperative Service Architecture

### Goal
Consolidate Fleece.Core's 19+ DI-registered services into a single `IFleeceService` that operates on a `.fleece/` folder. Internal business logic moves to static classes (functional core) that take data in and return results — no I/O, no state. The single service handles all I/O and orchestration.

### Architecture

```
CLI Commands
    │
    ▼
IFleeceService (single imperative service — manages storage, orchestrates operations)
    │
    ├── Issues.cs          (static: CRUD logic, filtering, search, graph building)
    ├── Dependencies.cs    (static: parent-child relationships, cycle detection, ordering)
    ├── Cleaning.cs        (static: tombstone creation, reference stripping)
    ├── Merging.cs         (static: multi-file deduplication, property-level merge)
    ├── Migration.cs       (static: schema migration logic)
    ├── IdGeneration.cs    (static: SHA256 ID generation)
    ├── Tags.cs            (static: tag parsing, validation, keyed tag operations)
    ├── Validation.cs      (static: cycle detection, schema validation)
    └── Search.cs          (static: query parsing, matching)
```

### Design Decisions

1. **Single service**: `IFleeceService` replaces `IIssueService`, `IDependencyService`, `ICleanService`, `IMergeService`, `IMigrationService`, `IDiffService`, `IValidationService`, `ISearchService`, and all factory/provider types.

2. **Functional core**: Each static class contains pure functions. Example: `Issues.Filter(IReadOnlyList<Issue> issues, FilterCriteria criteria)` returns filtered results without touching storage. This enables direct unit testing without mocks.

3. **Kept separate**:
   - `IGitService` — infrastructure concern, not core business logic. CLI uses it directly for `--commit`/`--push` flags.
   - `IFleeceInMemoryService` — caching/file-watching decorator. Wraps `IFleeceService` for consumers needing real-time reactivity (e.g., future server). Not all consumers need it.
   - `ISettingsService` — cross-cutting config concern. Passed to `FleeceService` constructor for identity resolution, but remains independently usable.

4. **Custom file support**: `FleeceService` constructor takes a base path (`.fleece/` folder). A static factory method `FleeceService.ForFile(path)` creates an instance scoped to a single JSONL file, replacing `IIssueServiceFactory`/`IStorageServiceProvider`.

5. **Storage is internal**: `IStorageService` becomes an internal implementation detail of `FleeceService`, not part of the public API. The `IJsonlSerializer` and `ISchemaValidator` also become internal.

6. **DI registration**: `AddFleeceCore()` registers only `IFleeceService`, `IGitService`, `ISettingsService`, and `IGitConfigService`. `AddFleeceInMemoryService()` additionally registers `IFleeceInMemoryService`.

### Verification Criteria
- All existing CLI commands work identically (no user-facing behavior changes)
- All existing tests pass (rewritten to test static functions directly where possible)
- External consumers (Homespun) can use `IFleeceService` as sole entry point
- `dotnet build` and `dotnet test` succeed
- No regressions in `fleece` CLI functionality

title: Phase 1: Extract functional core static classes

title: Extract Issues static class (filtering, search, graph building)

### Current State
- `IssueService` has: `FilterAsync`, `SearchAsync`, `BuildGraphAsync`, `QueryGraphAsync`, `BuildTaskGraphLayoutAsync`, `BuildFilteredTaskGraphLayoutAsync`, `GetNextIssuesAsync`, plus CRUD methods
- Filter/search/graph methods load all issues then operate in-memory
- CRUD methods interact with storage

### Target
Create `static class Issues` with pure functions:
- `static IReadOnlyList<Issue> Filter(IReadOnlyList<Issue> issues, IssueStatus? status, IssueType? type, int? priority, string? assignedTo, IReadOnlyList<string>? tags, int? linkedPr, bool includeTerminal)` 
- `static IReadOnlyList<Issue> Search(IReadOnlyList<Issue> issues, string query)` — text search across title/description/tags
- `static IssueGraph BuildGraph(IReadOnlyList<Issue> issues)` — builds parent-child graph
- `static IssueGraph QueryGraph(IReadOnlyList<Issue> issues, GraphQuery query, GraphSortConfig? sort)` — filtered subgraph
- `static TaskGraphLayout BuildTaskGraphLayout(IReadOnlyList<Issue> issues, InactiveVisibility visibility, string? assignedTo, GraphSortConfig? sort)`
- `static TaskGraphLayout BuildFilteredTaskGraphLayout(IReadOnlyList<Issue> issues, IReadOnlySet<string> matchedIds, GraphSortConfig? sort)`
- `static IReadOnlyList<Issue> GetNextIssues(IReadOnlyList<Issue> issues, string? parentId, GraphSortConfig? sort)`
- `static Issue ApplyCreate(string title, IssueType type, string id, string createdBy, ...)` — constructs a new Issue record (no I/O)
- `static Issue ApplyUpdate(Issue existing, ...)` — returns updated Issue with property-level timestamps

`IssueService` retains its async interface, loads issues from storage, delegates to `Issues.*`, then persists results for write operations.

### Files to Modify

- Modify: `src/Fleece.Core/Services/IssueService.cs` — delegate to static class

title: Extract Dependencies static class
description:
Extract dependency management logic from `DependencyService` into a static class `src/Fleece.Core/FunctionalCore/Dependencies.cs`.

### Current State

- Loads issues, manipulates ParentIssues collections, normalizes sibling rankings
- Uses `IValidationService` for cycle detection

### Target
Create `static class Dependencies` with pure functions:
- `static Issue AddDependency(Issue child, string parentId, IReadOnlyList<Issue> allIssues, DependencyPosition? position)` — returns updated child issue with new parent ref, throws if cycle detected
- `static Issue RemoveDependency(Issue child, string parentId)` — returns updated child without the parent ref
- `static (Issue moved, IReadOnlyList<Issue> siblings) MoveUp(Issue child, string parentId, IReadOnlyList<Issue> allIssues)` — returns updated child and affected siblings with reordered ranks
- `static (Issue moved, IReadOnlyList<Issue> siblings) MoveDown(Issue child, string parentId, IReadOnlyList<Issue> allIssues)` — same for down

These methods use `Validation.WouldCreateCycle(...)` for cycle detection (from the Validation extraction).

`DependencyService` retains its async interface, loads issues, delegates, persists.

title: Extract Cleaning static class

- The plan is a data object that the service applies (load → plan → persist)

The `CleanPlan` record/class should contain:
- `IReadOnlyList<Issue> IssuesToRemove`
- `IReadOnlyList<Tombstone> TombstonesToCreate`
- `IReadOnlyList<Issue> UpdatedIssues` (with dangling refs stripped)

`CleanService` loads data, calls `Cleaning.Plan(...)`, then persists the results.

title: Phase 2: Create IFleeceService and FleeceService
description:
Create the single `IFleeceService` interface and `FleeceService` implementation that consolidates all operations.

### Target

Create `src/Fleece.Core/Services/Interfaces/IFleeceService.cs` with a unified interface covering:

**Issue CRUD:**
- `CreateAsync(title, type, ...)` — creates issue, handles ID collision retry
- `UpdateAsync(id, ...)` — updates issue properties
- `DeleteAsync(id)` — soft delete
- `GetByIdAsync(id)` — get single issue
- `GetAllAsync()` — get all issues
- `ResolveByPartialIdAsync(partialId)` — resolve 3+ char partial IDs

**Filtering & Search:**
- `FilterAsync(status?, type?, priority?, assignedTo?, tags?, linkedPr?, includeTerminal?)` 
- `SearchAsync(query)` — text search
- `ParseSearchQuery(query)` — query DSL
- `SearchWithFiltersAsync(query, ...)` — search with CLI filter overrides
- `SearchWithContextAsync(query, ...)` — search with hierarchy context

**Graph Operations:**
- `BuildGraphAsync()` — full issue graph
- `QueryGraphAsync(query, sort?)` — filtered subgraph
- `BuildTaskGraphLayoutAsync(visibility, assignedTo?, sort?)` — task graph layout
- `BuildFilteredTaskGraphLayoutAsync(matchedIds, sort?)` — search result graph
- `GetNextIssuesAsync(parentId?, sort?)` — next actionable issues

**Dependencies:**
- `AddDependencyAsync(parentId, childId, position?)` 
- `RemoveDependencyAsync(parentId, childId)`
- `MoveUpAsync(parentId, childId)` / `MoveDownAsync(parentId, childId)`

**Maintenance:**
- `CleanAsync(...)` — clean deleted issues
- `MergeAsync(dryRun?)` — merge duplicate files
- `MigrateAsync()` / `IsMigrationNeededAsync()`
- `ValidateDependenciesAsync()` — cycle detection
- `HasMultipleUnmergedFilesAsync()` — check for unmerged state
- `GetIssueHierarchyAsync(id, includeParents?, includeChildren?)`
- `UpdateQuestionsAsync(id, questions)`

**Diagnostics:**
- `LoadIssuesWithDiagnosticsAsync()` — schema validation

### Implementation: `FleeceService`
- Constructor takes: `basePath` (string), `ISettingsService`, `IGitConfigService`
- Internally creates `JsonlStorageService` for I/O
- All methods follow: load from storage → delegate to static FunctionalCore classes → persist if write
- Static factory: `FleeceService.ForFile(filePath, settingsService, gitConfigService)` for single-file mode
- Thread-safe via internal `SemaphoreSlim` (carried over from storage)

### DI Registration
Update `ServiceCollectionExtensions.AddFleeceCore()`:
- Register `IFleeceService` → `FleeceService` 
- Keep old service registrations temporarily (removed in Phase 5)
- Register `IGitService`, `ISettingsService`, `IGitConfigService` as before

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create: `src/Fleece.Core/Services/Interfaces/IFleeceService.cs`
- Create: `src/Fleece.Core/Services/FleeceService.cs`
- Modify: `src/Fleece.Core/Extensions/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs` — register `IFleeceService`

### Verification
- `dotnet build` succeeds
- `dotnet test` passes
- Old services still work (not yet removed)
- New `IFleeceService` can be resolved from DI and all methods work correctly

title: Phase 3: Migrate CLI commands to IFleeceService
description:
Update all CLI commands to inject and use `IFleeceService` instead of the individual services (`IIssueServiceFactory`, `IStorageServiceProvider`, `IDependencyService`, etc.).

### Approach
Each command follows the same migration pattern:
1. Replace constructor parameters: remove `IIssueServiceFactory`, `IStorageServiceProvider`, `ISyncStatusService`, etc. → inject `IFleeceService` (and `IGitService` if needed for commit/push)
2. Replace `storageServiceProvider.GetStorageService(settings.IssuesFile)` → use `IFleeceService` directly (custom file support via a separate `IFleeceService` instance if needed)
3. Replace `issueServiceFactory.GetIssueService(...)` calls → `IFleeceService` methods
4. Replace `dependencyService.*` → `IFleeceService.AddDependencyAsync(...)` etc.
5. Keep output formatting unchanged

### Verification
- `dotnet build` succeeds  
- `dotnet test` passes
- All `fleece` CLI commands produce identical output to before
- Commands using `--issues-file` still work

title: Migrate CRUD commands (Create, Edit, Delete, Show)

- **DeleteCommand** — uses `IIssueServiceFactory`, `IStorageServiceProvider`. Replace with `IFleeceService.DeleteAsync(...)`.
- **ShowCommand** — uses `IIssueServiceFactory`, `IStorageServiceProvider`. Replace with `IFleeceService.GetByIdAsync(...)` / `GetIssueHierarchyAsync(...)`.

### Pattern
For each command:
1. Change constructor to inject `IFleeceService` (and `IGitService` if commit/push flags are used)
2. Remove `IIssueServiceFactory`, `IStorageServiceProvider` injections
3. Replace `storageServiceProvider.GetStorageService(settings.IssuesFile)` + `issueServiceFactory.GetIssueService(settings.IssuesFile)` with direct `IFleeceService` calls

- `src/Fleece.Cli/Commands/EditCommand.cs`
- `src/Fleece.Cli/Commands/DeleteCommand.cs`
- `src/Fleece.Cli/Commands/ShowCommand.cs`

title: Migrate List, Search, Next commands

Migrate the query/display CLI commands to use `IFleeceService`.

### Commands to Migrate

`ListCommand` uses `ISyncStatusService` for sync status display. This service should be kept as a separate injection for now (it's infrastructure, not core business logic). It can be folded into `IFleeceService` if desired, but is not required for this task.

title: Migrate maintenance commands (Clean, Merge, Migrate, Validate, Diff)
description:
Migrate the maintenance CLI commands to use `IFleeceService`.

### Commands to Migrate
- **CleanCommand** — uses `ICleanService`. Replace with `IFleeceService.CleanAsync(...)`.
- **MergeCommand** — uses `IMergeService`. Replace with `IFleeceService.MergeAsync(...)`.
- **MigrateCommand** — uses `IMigrationService`. Replace with `IFleeceService.MigrateAsync()`, `IsMigrationNeededAsync()`.

Recommended: add `CompareFilesAsync(file1, file2)` to `IFleeceService` that delegates to the static `Diff` functions, OR keep `IDiffService` as a standalone utility. Choose whichever is simpler.

title: Phase 4: Update tests for functional core
description:
Add unit tests for the new static functional core classes and update existing tests.

### Approach

**New tests for static classes:**
For each static class in `src/Fleece.Core/FunctionalCore/`, create corresponding test files in `tests/Fleece.Core.Tests/FunctionalCore/`:
- `IdGenerationTests.cs` — test ID generation directly (move relevant tests from `Sha256IdGeneratorTests`)
- `TagsTests.cs` — test tag operations directly (move from `TagServiceTests`)
- `ValidationTests.cs` — test cycle detection with in-memory issue lists (move from `ValidationServiceTests`)
- `IssuesTests.cs` — test filter/search/graph with in-memory data (move from `IssueServiceTests`, `IssueGraphServiceTests`)
- `DependenciesTests.cs` — test dependency ops with in-memory data (move from `DependencyServiceTests`)
- `CleaningTests.cs` — test clean planning with in-memory data (move from `CleanServiceTests`)
- `MergingTests.cs` — test merge logic with in-memory data (move from `MergeServiceTests`, `IssueMergerTests`)
- `MigrationTests.cs` — test migration logic (move from `MigrationServiceTests`)
- `SearchTests.cs` — test search (move from `SearchServiceTests`)

**Key benefit:** These tests require NO mocks — just construct issue lists and call static methods. This is the primary win of the functional core pattern.

**Existing integration tests:** Keep `FleeceInMemoryServiceIntegrationTests`, `CleanIntegrationTests` as-is since they test the full stack. Update them to use `IFleeceService` if they currently use individual services.

**Remove old service tests:** Once functional core tests cover the same logic, delete the old service-level tests that were just testing the same logic through a DI wrapper.

- Code coverage for functional core classes is at least as good as before

title: Phase 5: Remove old service interfaces and clean up DI
description:
Remove the old individual service interfaces and implementations that have been replaced by `IFleeceService` + static functional core.

### Services to Remove
Delete these interfaces and their implementations:
- `IIssueService` / `IssueService` — replaced by `IFleeceService` + `Issues` static class
- `IDependencyService` / `DependencyService` — replaced by `IFleeceService` + `Dependencies` static class
- `ICleanService` / `CleanService` — replaced by `IFleeceService` + `Cleaning` static class
- `IMergeService` / `MergeService` — replaced by `IFleeceService` + `Merging` static class
- `IMigrationService` / `MigrationService` — replaced by `IFleeceService` + `Migration` static class
- `IValidationService` / `ValidationService` — replaced by `IFleeceService` + `Validation` static class
- `IDiffService` / `DiffService` — replaced by `IFleeceService` or kept standalone (see Phase 3 decision)
- `ISearchService` / `SearchService` — replaced by `IFleeceService` + `SearchOps` static class
- `IIdGenerator` / `Sha256IdGenerator` — replaced by `IdGeneration` static class
- `ITagService` / `TagService` — replaced by `Tags` static class
- `IIssueServiceFactory` / `IssueServiceFactory` — replaced by `FleeceService.ForFile(...)`
- `IStorageServiceProvider` / `StorageServiceProvider` — internal to `FleeceService`
- `ISyncStatusService` / `SyncStatusService` — evaluate: fold into `IFleeceService` or keep

### Internalize Storage
- Make `IStorageService` / `JsonlStorageService` `internal` (not public API)
- Make `IJsonlSerializer` / `JsonlSerializer` `internal`
- Make `ISchemaValidator` / `SchemaValidator` `internal`

### Update DI Registration
Simplify `AddFleeceCore()` to register only:
- `IFleeceService` → `FleeceService`
- `IGitService` → `GitService`
- `ISettingsService` → `SettingsService`
- `IGitConfigService` → `GitConfigService`
- Any internal services needed by `FleeceService` (storage, serializer)

Simplify `AddFleeceInMemoryService()` to wrap `IFleeceService`.

### Remove Old Tests
Delete test files for removed services (already replaced in Phase 4):
- `IssueServiceTests.cs`, `DependencyServiceTests.cs`, `CleanServiceTests.cs`, etc.
- Keep integration tests and storage tests

### Files to Delete
- All files in `src/Fleece.Core/Services/Interfaces/` for removed services
- All corresponding implementation files in `src/Fleece.Core/Services/`
- Old test files in `tests/Fleece.Core.Tests/Services/` that are now redundant

### Files to Modify
- `src/Fleece.Core/Extensions/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs` — simplified registration
- Any remaining references to old interfaces

### Verification
- `dotnet build` succeeds with no warnings about unused code
- `dotnet test` passes
- `IFleeceService` is the only public service interface (besides `IGitService`, `ISettingsService`, `IGitConfigService`, `IFleeceInMemoryService`)
- No CLI command references any removed interface

title: Make ID generation non-deterministic - IDs should be random
description:
The generation of IDs was previously meant to be deterministic, then a salt was added to make it optionally non-deterministic. See the tests in tests/Fleece.Core.Tests/FunctionalCore/IdGenerationTests.cs for more information on this.

Change the overall ID generation function to always generate a random ID for an issue that is not related to any other property of the issue. This should be done by generating a GUID and taking the first 6 characters if they don't clash with any other IDs that have already been created. The clash detection should be done by the FleeceService and not the ID generation functional core.

title: Review last-write-wins properties
description: The `fleece merge` command uses a last-write-wins system such that only the latest changes are applied to all individual properties of an issue. Some properties appear to have been added without this behaviour considered, however, resulting in unpredictable merge behaviour. Review the overall JSON schema and implementation to confirm that all properties have this enabled.

title: Fix merge issues with parent-issues field
description:
There are times when I have made an edit to the parent issues field of an issue, but when I merge in another issues file containing older changes the new edit that I had made is overwritten with the previous edit. Please add a test to ensure that any changes to the parent issues field persist after merging to confirm that this functionality works. The entire list of parent issues should be considered as part of the "state" of the issue managed by the "parentIssuesLastUpdate" field.

After adding the test, if it fails find the issue and try to fix it.

title: Fix flat rendering of list --tree and list --next for legacy parent refs
description: PR #113 introduced per-ref Active/LastUpdated/UpdatedBy on ParentIssueRef but System.Text.Json source-gen does not honor the = true initializer on Active. Legacy JSONL parent refs (missing the new fields) deserialize with Active=false and disappear from Issue.ActiveParentIssues, collapsing tree/next rendering to a flat list. Fix: auto-run Migration.Migrate in FleeceService.LoadAndNormalizeAsync.

title: Remove TUI
description: When `fleece` is run without any commands, a TUI is started. However, this TUI doesn't work currently - just remove it entirely from the codebase.

title: Use skills rather than the prime hook
description: The `fleece install` command currently installs a hook that runs `fleece prime` on startup. Instead of this, I want to move the `fleece prime` content into a skill and have the `fleec install` command install that.

title: Remove the external text edit feature
Add linked-pr to the edit command
